column_description: Unique identifier of fulfillment. If external system does not provide a value then the system creates a business key."
Field on the entity to store purge date)
The source from which the fulfillment originated. It could be bridged in from external or from order component.
The first name of the Customer.
The last name of the customer.
Email of the customer.
Phone Number of the customer.
The location from where the fulfillment is executed.
Ship-to location Id, in case of ship to store or any flow where the items are shipping to a destination which is configured as a location in the system.
Latest possible shipping timestamp on which the items should be shipped out from store.
Delivery date which was originally promised to the customer.

Ship via which is requested to fulfill the particular fulfillment order line. Ship Via Id is a unique combination of Carrier Code and Service Level Code. Either Carrier Code and Service Level Code or Ship Via Id is mandatory. If both are populated then Ship Via Id takes precedence over the Carrier Code and Service Level Code populated in the Fulfillment.

Service Level which should be used to fulfill the order line. Either Carrier Code and Service Level Code or Ship Via Id is mandatory. If both are populated then Ship Via Id takes precedence over the Carrier Code and Service Level Code populated in the Fulfillment.
Carrier used to ship the fulfillment to the customer.
Indicates  the lowest status of among all the fulfillment lines of a given fulfillment. Example: pending pickup, packed, shipped etc.
Indicates the highest status, excluding Cancelled, among the fulfillment lines of a given fulfillment. Example: pending pickup, packed, shipped etc.
Defines the type of document for the order. Used to differentiate retail, customer, and B2B orders. Propagated from order.
Order Type used by order component for order processing.This is propagated from Order.

Indicates the method of fulfillment.  This decides how the fulfillment will be processed in the store.  When active omni order management is used, this information flows from release entity. Replenishment to store from DC should be mapped as DCtoStore. Valid values include DCtoStore, ShipToLocation, ShipToAddress, ShipToStore and PickUpAtStore.
This is used when the delivery method is ship to store to differentiate between  merge and pickup orders.
This is the date when the fulfillment gets shipped.
This is the calculate date from the carrier for when the delivery of the order could be expected.
This is the actual order captured date and denoted the date when the customer placed the order.Earliest date if orders are consolidated.

Indicates the priority determined by the Fulfillment component based on the Prioritization Tags configured in the system. If no Prioritization Tags are configured, then Priority is the same as the Order Priority.
Indicates the priority of the order which was set by the order component.
Indicates the total order value.
Indicates if the fulfillment is a gift.
Indicates if the fulfillment needs value added services. Even if one fulfillment line needs VAS, this flag is set to true.
Indicates if the fulfillment has any alerts.
Indicates if the fulfillment has any instructions.
Location id to locate the return center
Indicates if the fulfillment needs return label.
Indicates if the fulfillment/ASN is received or not. If all packages are not received by the time of updating this flag, remaining packages will be made missing.
Expiry date of the Customer Pickup order.
Indicates the total value of the units that is purchased in addition to the original items
Indicates the total value of items that are picked up by the customer
This flag indicates if packing is complete for a pullbacks/transfers or store initiated transfer order.

Indicates whether we are allowing over packing for the pullback orders or not, if OverageAllowed is set to true and order type is Pullback then user will be allowed to ship or pack more than order quantity for any line. If OverageAllowed is set to false, user will not be allowed to ship or pack more than order quantity.
Indicates if the fulfillment is a parcel or non-parcel.
Unique ID used to identify the customer

This specifies the locale in which the order was captured. If item attributes such as description, style, etc. are null on order creation, the attributes will be populated in this locale if translations are available in the item component. If no translations exist for the specified locale, then the default item attributes are saved. This field cannot be edited once an fulfillment is created.Valid values are locale codes supported in base like en, es, pt, etc.
Indicates if the fulfillment should be picked in a slotted cart or not

This field represents the organization in which the order is captured. This will be populated only when the ordering organization is different from the fulfilling store’s organization. This field should not be populated for other kinds of fulfillments like replenishments, pullbacks etc
Currency Code of the order sent from in the release from OM. This field represents the currency in which the order is placed.
This is an additional classification with delivery methods. This differentiates orders between in-store pickup vs curbside pickup and similarly ship to address vs send sale orders.
This indicates when the customer has arrived in the parking area to pick up the order.

This attribute keeps the original value of delivery method subtype when the order was released to fulfillment. Because the other attribute i.e. ‘Delivery method subtype’ value can get change if the fulfillment parameter ‘Capture the actual pickup type of the pickup at store order during the pickup’ is enabled.
Indicates that the customer is on their way to the store. It is used within the store application to differentiate between Arrived and On the Way curbside pickup orders.
Indicates customer’s expected time of arrival at the store. The pickupETADateTime is calculated by the updatePickupFulfillmentsAndNotify API if PickupETA is passed as input.
Indicates if the fulfillment needs to be shipped using a same day delivery carrier.
Indicates if the fulfillment was converted from a pickup order to a shipment order.
Indicates whether the fulfillment is eligible for conversion from pick up at store to ship to address.
Defines the transfer type for a store initiated transfer.

Indicates if the fulfillment is created against a Reserve Online and Pickup in Store order. These orders are Pickup in Store orders which are created without any payment information and are paid for at the time of their pickup.

In the case of external POS integration, ROPIS orders are assumed to be paid if the Store Associate acknowledges the popup which ensues on the tap of the Confirm Pickup button. It is the Store Associate’s responsibility to ensure that the required amount is paid using an external register before they acknowledge the popup. In the case of internal POS integrations, Active POS handles the balance due payment and once the payment is successful, this flag is updated to true. A true value implies that the payment is received in full. This property is meant for auditing purposes only and it doesn’t update the Payment Header in the payment component.
This indicates the sum of the shorted quantities of fulfillment lines.
This indicates the total time taken to complete picking process in minutes.
This indicates the total time taken to complete packing process in minutes.
This indicates whether the fulfillment was picked on time or not with respect to the defined goal time.
The User ID of the associate who performed picking process.
The User ID of the associate who performed packing process.
Date and time before which fulfillment operations should be completed based on store hours.
Boolean field specifies whether a fulfillment is in picking stage or not.
cancel reason code for the cancelled fulfillment

Not used for any processing logic in SIF. It is just a pass-through field that is persisted. This is the allocation object type from order promising.
Id of the state transition pipeline.

Unique identifier of the ASN. Applicable only to ship to store fulfillment lines which are allocated against in-transit inventory. If this is blank in the order release, then the fulfillment is converted from STS-Pickup to BOPIS.
Indicates the actual unit price of the item. This does not include the tax or discounts applied on the item
Indicates if the line is returned to the shelf
Original quantity picked by store
Number of times fulfillment line has been shorted (backordered) from other locations

Denotes if the customer has permitted substitution of the item that they have ordered. Accepts three values: ''DoNotSubstitute'', ''BestAvailable'', and ''SpecificItem''. ''DoNotSubstitute'' means that the customer hasn''t allowed substitution of the ordered item. ''BestAvailable'' implies that substitution is allowed in line with the configured substitution rules in Active Omni, an external OMS, or based on the store associate''s discretion. ''SpecificItem'' denotes that substitution is allowed only from the customer specified items.

Denotes the types of substitutions which are to be enabled in the UI. NONE means this line cannot be substituted, BASIC means this line can be substituted from the list of available items, FREEFORM means that the line can be substituted by any configured item using the context menu, ALL means the line can be substituted either from the list of available items or any configured item using the context menu.

This temporary property is meant to be used only during reprint pickupslip for a fulfillment that involves an item which has been substituted. During reprint, scanning a substituted item should reprint the pickup slip for that fufillment. This will be deprecated in future.

Package, Item or load. Receiving process honors the receipt type and enables the receiving to happen by scanning the package or item. If the receipt type is load, store associate can receive the entire shipment by scanning only one of the packages.

It is used to store the previous receipt type of the package whose inventory is adjusted during the receiving process. Its default value is null and it remains so if the package is received without any adjustments during receipt.
Customer Pickup or Store Inventory
Used in Ship to Store. The pickup leg fulfillment to which this package belongs

This is the status of the shipment/package delivery from the carrier and it is updated when the shipment/package is delivered by the carrier at the store.

It is the Order ID which is received, in the Fulfillment, from the OMS. It is populated for the Ship to Address, Ship to Store merge legs, Pullbacks and Transfers, and Replenishments related Packages. In the UI, it is displayed only for those Packages whose fulfillments have the Order ID available.
Indicates whether the package is late shipment after the package already received in the store.

Captures User ID of the Store Associate who closes a Store Initiated Transfer, Corporate Transfer, or a Customer Shipment Package. It is used to display “Packed By” Store Associate’s name in Completed Shipments UI.

Identifies whether a package is a Pullback / Corporate Transfer, Store Initiated Transfer, or a Customer Shipment. All Ship to Address and Ship to Store merge leg packages are classified as Customer Shipments. The Shipment Type is displayed in the Completed Shipments UI and is also available as a filtering option there.

Priority of order, used in case of constrained inventory

If set true ,it indicates that the order has been originally captured off-line i.e. Edge Mode. If set false then order has been captured on-line.

If isCancelled is true, these are comments entered for the cancellation
Date of initial order capture from the order capture system
Indicates if order is tax exempt
If isTaxExempt is true for this order, then this is the taxexemptid which represents the tax excempt reason
If isTaxExempt is true for this order, these are the comments entered
Currency code to be used for processing the order payment
Indicates if order is ready to be tendered.  If true, order component will invoke payment component to process payment.
Date of the order confirmation
Indicates if the tax has been overridden
Sum of all charges for the order and order lines
Sum of all discounts for the order and order lines
Sum of all taxes for the order and order lines

It has the tax override value. The value can be in terms of percentage or flat number and the precision is upto 2 places of decimal. When there is a need to provide a percentage for taxOverrideValue which requires higher precision (upto 3 or 4 places of decimal, say 2.3456), then donot use this attribute instead use taxOverridePercValue

It has the tax override value in percentage. This attribute can have values with a precision upto 4 places of decimal. Hence when the tax override percentage requires higher precision, then this attribute must be used.
Sum of all order line subtotals where each order line subtotal is the item price times the order line quantity.
Sum of items, charges, discounts, and taxes for the order and order lines
Sum of items, charges, discounts, and taxes for the order and order lines at the time of confirmation
Indicates if the order is post-voided

Identifier of existing reservation.  If inventory is already reserved for this order, the reservationRequestId for the reservation is populated here so that Promising can allocate this order against these reserved quantities
This column used by monitoring framework to set the time at which the immediate/next event has to occur
Date on which order would be archived post completion or cancellation
Total number of non-cancelled lines having deliveryMethod as StoreSale
Total number of lines including Cancelled line having devliveryMethod as ShipToAddress or ShipToStore or PickUpAtStore or Email
Total number of non-cancelled lines having deliveryMethod as StoreReturn
Count of order lines where isReturn = true. Used for displaying return order lines in the DS UI and to identify orders where any return items exist
Total number of cancelled lines in order

Number of packages the customer will be shipping to the return center. Used to request labels from the carrier. For instance, if the customer indicates that two packages are required for shipping their return items, then the CSR inputs 2 in the call center UI, and 2 labels are retrieved from the carrier. The email marketing system emails these two labels to the customer to affix to the return shipments. Required only when delivery method is Ship to Return Center

Email address to which the return shipping labels should be sent. During call center return creation, this is captured and input by the CSR. Required only when delivery method is Ship to Return Center
Store''s Business Date on which the order is created.  This can be different from the capture date if the store is opened through more than one calendar date.
Indicates if the order is under going archival process

This property is introduced to handle unArchive failure scenarios.UnArchiving is a multi step process, if any step is failed after Order is loaded from search, this flag value set to true, so that on next unArchive process, it tries to resume the remaining process
Indicates the minimum status of all Return lines within Order. It consider all the lines for which IsReturn is true and among such lines this is the minimum status.
Indicates the maximum status of all Return lines within Order. It consider all the lines for which IsReturn is true and among such lines this is the maximum status.
Indicates the minimum fulfillment lifecycle status of all units on the order
Indicates the maximum fulfillment lifecycle status of all units on the order
Identifies the Suspended order ID  which is  resumed in the current order.  It should be populated only on the resumed order.

Order quantities cannot be released before this date and time. This value can be imported on the order, or it can be calculated by the system as a specified amount of time after an order has been allocated. To specify the amount of time, this can be configured on the milestone expected time rule.
Comments provided by user when a return order is processed by a CSR to expedite refund processing or an exchange shipment.

This field indicates the total merchandise items (Orderline.isNonMerchandise=False) sold in an order, with delivery option=In store sale,Ship to address/Ship to store/Pick up . The value should get incremented when an In store line is added, decremented if canceled or removed or delivery option changes.

This field indicates the total merchandise items (Orderline.isNonMerchandise=False) returned at store. This is not determined by the delivery option. Items bought across channels, returned at store are considered. The value should get incremented when an line is added where isReturn=True. Decremented when the line is voided or cancelled.
Sum of all cancelled lines'' SubTotal. If the entire order gets cancelled, OrderSubTotal value is moved to this field, Order. OrderSubTotal becomes 0.
Sum of the discounts applied on cancelled lines. If the entire order gets cancelled, Order.totalDiscounts is set to 0, value is moved to this field.
This column is used by milestone scheduler to set the time at which it picked the order for processing
This Column is used to check whether to count this Order
This column is used for storing the date when the order is confirmed
Sum of all cancelled order total. If the entire order gets cancelled, OrderTotal value is moved to this field, Order. OrderTotal becomes 0.
This attribute is to capture the loyalty number of the customer on the order, this data can be used in user exits to validate with 3rd party application and come back with rewards to the customer.
This attribute is used to persist customer signature captured at point of sale.
Indicates a unique Id for every bulk order imports. System populates this when import bulk orders is triggered. This field also used on Order UI to render the list of matching orders per Run Id

This specifies the locale in which the order is captured. If item attributes such as description, style, etc. are null on order creation, the attributes will be populated in this locale if translations are available in the item component. If no translations exist for the specified locale, then the default item attributes are saved. This field cannot be edited once an order is created.Valid values are locale codes supported in base like en, es, pt, etc.

Random number for the order, generated by the system. Used for authentication for Digital Self-Service. For example, if a customer wants to access self-service shipment tracking, the self-service URL contains orderId, orgId, and this token, and these attributes are validated against the order to authenticate the customer before rendering the self-service UI. This token is automatically generated upon order creation for all orders. If the order was created before token generation was enabled, then this token is generated upon receiving status updates from fulfillment systems (e.g. ''Picked'' status update).

Random number for the order, generated by the system. Used for authentication for Digital Self-Service. For example, if a customer wants to access self-service shipment tracking, the self-service URL contains orderId, orgId, and this token, and these attributes are validated against the order to authenticate the customer before rendering the self-service UI. This token is automatically generated upon order creation for all orders. If the order was created before token generation was enabled, then this token is generated upon receiving status updates from fulfillment systems (e.g. ''Picked'' status update).
Version identifier for order, to prevent stale updates

This field indicates the money owed either by the retailer or by the customer. For example, if a customer places an order of $100 and prepays it via cash, and the order is not yet shipped, then the value of this field will be 100. When $20 worth of items are shipped, then the value becomes 80. Another example is when a customer places an order of $100 via credit card, and the settlement transaction fails after shipment, then the value of this field will be -100.
This field indicates that the particular return is printerless return.
DEPRECATED. PLEASE USE AwaitingPayForPBLOrd attribute

This is used for PayByLink orders to track whether payment is captured from a customer. For example, when a PayByLink request is sent to the customer, the status will be set to ''PaymentRequested''. Once payment is received from the customer, the status will be updated to ''PaymentReceived''. If the payment is not received from the customer within the configured number of hours, the status will be updated to ''PaymentNotReceived''. By default, the status is set to ''Null''.
This attribute is used to persist the sum of Order Informational Taxes and all the Order Line Informational Taxes.
This attribute is to capture the printerless return option selected by the customer during return creation.
Sum of all cancelled taxes for the order
Sum of all cancelled charges for the order
Order Type used for order processing. If not provided, then the default Order Type is used.  Valid values are defined in the orderType entity.
Defines the type of document for the order.  This is used to differentiate retail, customer, and B2B orders.
This specifies the channel where the transaction was captured.  For example, this could be ''Store'' or ''Web''
If isCancelled is true, this field indicates the cancel reason code
If isTaxOverridden is true, the tax override reason code
If isTaxExempt is true for this order, this field indicates the tax exempt reason code.
If isPostVoided is true, this field indicates the post-void cancel reason code
Status of the order within the payment lifecycle; only can be imported if payment is managed outside OM

Indicates whether the refund should be issued via the original payment method, or via a new gift card. Used during return invoice creation to determine whether to refund the customer via the original payment method, or to refund the gift recipient via a new gift card order line.
Indicates if the invoice has been published to external systems

Reason provided by user when a return order is processed by a CSR to expedite refund processing or an exchange shipment. When a return order is processed, then the return quantity moves to Returned status and a return invoice is created. Any exchange line holds with hold type Return Items Pending are resolved, and exchange lines are released for fulfillment.

Indicates whether a POS customer wants to be refunded via the original payment method or a new payment method in case of a return. If refundPaymentMethod = New, then the payment component does not transfer return credit from the parent order. If refundPaymentMethod = Original, then payment moves return credit in the payment summary and copies payment methods and transactions, so that a refund can be issued via the original payment method.
If the Tax is overridden for this OrderLine, then the value for this property should be selected either ''FlatAmount'' or ''Percentage''

Indicates the maximum fulfillment lifecycle status of all units on the order line
Priority of order line, used in case of constrained inventory
If value is yes, this indicates the item is not stored in the Item master

Indicates if item is returnable. Can be used by return capture systems, such as the call center UI, POS UI, or any third party return capture system. If IsReturanble = false, then the UI should display the item as non-returnable but could optionally allow the user to override this and return the item anyways. This field is not used by the order component to validate returnability during return order creation; any restrictions must be done by the UI.

Indicates if item is returnable at Store. This field is intended to be used by store return capture systems (e.g. POS) to indicate if an item is returnable. IsReturnableAtStore is used to set orderLine IsReturnable flag at the time of order creation. If IsReturnableAtStore and IsReturnableAtDC are false, orderLine IsReturnable is set to false. If either one is True, order line is flagged as returnable. The POS UI  typically provides the ability to override this field (based on permissions) - for example, if an item is ''final sale'' and is not returnable, then the POS user will see that the item is not returnable, but they have the ability to override the item to returnable in exception scenarios. There is no validation of this field on the back end upon return order creation, as this is not a hard validation but is intended for use in the UI.

Indicates if item is eligible to be exchagned. Can be used by return capture systems, such as the call center UI, POS UI, or any third party return capture system. If isExchangeable = false, then the UI should display the item as non-exchangeable but could optionally allow the user to override this and exchange the item anyways. This field is not used by the order component to validate during exchange order creation; any restrictions must be done by the UI.
Indicates if item is a gift

This flag indicates if a gift card line is added by the system to process any refunds resulting from order updates where the payment was originally made by cash or gift cards. Order updates can include order cancellation, applying appeasement, regular returns resulting in refunds or a gift receipt returns
Indicates if the order line is on hold
Indicates if item is pre-order
Indicates if item is pre-sale
Indicates date on which pre-order item can be shipped/sold
IIndicates if the order line is cancelled
If isCancelled is true, these are comments entered for the cancellation

Identifies the original order line in case of returns or even exchanges. Used in conjunction with parent order id while retrieving prices, charges, taxes, discounts, etc. from the original order line to be copied on return or exchange lines. Parent order id and parent order line id should only be populated in return or even exchange scenarios, since return credit is pulled from the parent order and applied to the return or exchange order. Parent order line id should not be populated for sale lines or uneven exchange lines
ID used to associate a payment with an item or group of items

Original UnitPrice, if the price is overridden
Indicates if tax is included in the UnitPrice, in case of a Value-Added Tax (VAT) or a return charge.
Indicates if the line tax has been overridden

It has the tax override value. The value can be in terms of percentage or flat number and the precision is upto 2 places of decimal. When there is a need to provide a percentage for taxOverrideValue which requires higher precision (upto 3 or 4 places of decimal, say 2.3456), then donot use this attribute instead use taxOverridePercValue

It has the tax override value in percentage. This attribute can have values with a precision upto 4 places of decimal. Hence when the tax override percentage requires higher precision, then this attribute must be used.
Indicates if the price has been overridden
Earliest date on which the order line should be shipped; This is read only if IsPackAndHold is true.
Indicates if the order line should be held until the DoNotShipBeforeDate

Requested date on which the items should be delivered to the destination. *Example: 2015-10-28T10:15:30 (ISO Date Time Format)*. If the RDD is added on the order/promising request then the system will validate if a transit lane exists for the order origin and destination. If no transit lane exist or if we the system cannot find an origin location to fulfill the order in time then the allocation will fail. If RDD is null on the order/promising request and the system does not find a transit lane, allocation will still succeed but transit time will be null. Adding a RDD ensures backward scheduling dates are computed.
Delivery date which was originally promised to the customer

Latest possible delivery date on which the items should be delivered to the destination. Used by SIF to display At risk tag on late orders. Passthrough field for OM. If RDD is used in the implementation, should be mapped with the same value as Requested delivery date.

Delivery Method Sub Type selected by the customer. For example, sub type ‘Curbside’ indicates the customer wants to receive items in their car in the parking lot for delivery method PickUpAtStore. Delivery Method Sub type is used by fulfillment system when completing the pick up/shipment flow.
Carrier which should be used to fulfill the order line e.g. Fedex, UPS etc
Service Level which should be used to fulfill the order line e.g. AIR-1 Day, Road-day
Rank of the order line, as calculated by the system during prioritization.
Identifier of the shipping address, which is stored in the address table. Used if deliveryMethod is Ship to address

Ship from address for return order lines being shipped from a customer address to a return center. This address is printed on return labels and used to calculate return taxes. Ship from address can be specified on return order import. If null on import and delivery method is Ship to Return Center, then ship from address is populated with the ship to address from the parent order line. If parent order line ship to address is null (in case of pick up in store or ship to store), then the preferred customer address is retrieved from the customer record. If no preferred customer address exists, then ship from address should be manually entered by a user
Ship-to location ID, in case of ship to store, pick up in store, or any flow where the items are shipping to a destination which is configured as a location in the network
ID used to group items which need to be fulfilled together (e.g. warranties, kits, gift with purchase, etc.)

If release configuration is enabled for Deliver Together. ReleaseGroupId can be populated to act as an override for forming release group. For instance: if an order with four lines have same Fulfillment Group but releaseGroupId is 1 for first 3 lines whereas 2 for last line then there will be two groups instead of one.

Customer shipping preference. When a customer order is placed, it is required to know what is his preference of shipping. For example, it could be Same day delivery / Next Day / Two day or prefers ground shipment
Indicates if the item is perishable

Indicates if the item is being returned. If isReturn = true and an order has not yet been priced, then return price, taxes, and charges are pulled from the parent order line. For example, if sale line has unit price $200, $10 tax, and $8 SnH, then when a return line is created against this parent order line, the return line unit price is set to -$200, with -$10 line tax and -$8 SnH. If isReturn = true and no parent order line is found, then the current price and tax rates are used to calculate the refund price and tax. If isReturn = false, then the line is sent through pricing, promo, tax, and SnH per the typical sale flow, unless the line is an even exchange (see isEvenExchange)
Selling location of order line, in case of multi-company orders or shared carts. This needs to be the location ID of a store existing in the system.

Identifies the original order in case of returns or even exchanges. Parent order is used in conjunction with parent order line id while retrieving prices, charges, taxes, discounts, etc. from the original order line to be copied on return or exchange lines. Parent order id and parent order line id should only be populated in return or even exchange scenarios, since return credit is pulled from the parent order and applied to the return or exchange order. Parent order id should not be populated for sale lines or uneven exchange lines

Location from where the items are being fulfilled. Populated with the allocation origin location for ship to home items. Populated with the selling location for store sales. Populated with the ship to location for pick up in store and ship to store. Used to send the origin address to the tax engine, which calculates tax rates based on this data
Tax code of the ordered item

This is the volumetric weight of an item and the same is used to calculate Shipping and Handling charges when the rate basis for Shipping and Handling charges calculation is configured as Volumetric weight
Product Class of Item.
Id of the state transition pipeline.

Identifies items being exchanged for an item of the same style, but in a different color or size. If isEvenExchange = true and rePriceEvenExchanges is disabled, then line price, taxes, and charges are copied from the parent order line. For example, if the original line has unit price $200, $10 tax, and $8 SnH, then an even exchange line would be created with unit price is set to $200, with $10 line tax and $8 SnH. If isEvenExchange = true and no parent order line is found or parent order line is null, then an error is thrown. If isEvenExchange = true, then isReturn cannot be set to true. If isEvenExchange = false or rePriceEvenExchanges is enabled, then the line is sent through pricing, promo, tax, and SnH per the typical sale flow.

For return order lines where the delivery method is ship to return center, this flag indicates if receipt of the return item is expected by the retailer. If true, then the customer plans to ship the item back to the retailer''s return center. If false, then the customer does not return any item to the retailer (aka field destroy). If receipt expected is false, then the retailer can decide to approve the return and issue a refund or to reject the return and issue no refund. The return configuration autoApproveReceiptNotExpected indicates whether these order lines are automatically approved on order confirmation or that a CSR needs to manually approve these lines.

If return order lines with receiptedExpected as true is changed to false due to threshold validation then this flag will be updated to true. This allows selling channel to keep track of lines that were updated by the system.
This flag indicates its a gift card item
This flag indicates its a non merchandise item
Unique identifier of the order with which the OrderLine is associated
Indicates if the item qualifies as hazmat
Hazardous Material code populated from hazardousMaterialId attribute of the item
When an order line is cancelled, populate this field with Orderline.OrderLineSubTotal value.
When an order line is cancelled, populate this field with Orderline.TotalDiscounts value.

Department number of the item. Ensure this value is defined as an integer, if this attribute needs to be copied to the order line entity. For example, when an order is created with item A, if item A has department number 678 defined, then this department number is saved on the order line.

Department number of the item. The value of this attribute is copied from Item to the order line entity. For example, when an order is created with Item A which belongs to the department number “Softline123”, then this department number is copied and saved in the order line which contains the Item A.
DepartmentName of the ordered item.
This field will capture the X-ref info when we have added item using alternate UPC or X-ref
This flag indicates if tax exempt is applicable on the item. by default all items will have this value set to true.
Indicates if item is gift card item needs to be activated freshly or if its an reload to an existing gift card
This field indicates the max amount of discount in percentage that can be applied to the item in the orderline.
This field indicates the maxBalanceDue amount of discount in money that can be applied to the item in orderline.
This flag indicates if tax exempt is applicable on the item. by default all items have this value set to true.
Store''s Business Date which is set in Back Office. This can be different from the transaction created date if the store is kept open through more than one calendar date.
Indicates transaction id under which this orderline is captured. eg Adding more store sale lines in existing order while customer comes to pick up in a store.

This field will capture Sum of all the discount against Item, It can be discount applied directly on Item or prorated Order discount or prorated discount on entire line. It can be of any Charge Type, Promotion, Coupon, Appeasement or Discount.
Created timestamp of the parent order line. Used in return invoice tax requests to pass the original parent order line created date to the third party tax engine, so that tax holidays can be honored.
Ship date which was originally promised to the customer during order creation

This flag indicates if the order line has component items defined. This is a calculated field, if there is at least one OrderLineComponents entity for the current line then this flag should set to true.
Identifies the allocation config which is used to allocate the order line. Used by the reapportionment scheduler to pick order lines for a given config id, when the config scheduler runs.

Indicates if an order line can have manual discounts applied against it. E.g. header discounts applied at POS should not be prorated to gift card items. When a header-level charge of type Discount is applied, if the chargeTypeOverride table has skipNonDiscountableItems = true for Discounts, then the header discount is only prorated to lines where isDiscountable = true. If the chargeTypeOverride config does not have skipNonDiscountableItems = true for Discounts, then header discounts are prorated across all order lines, including non-discountable items. Validations for line-level discounts should be handled in order capture systems; there is no validation of line level discounts saved against order lines which are not discountable. This logic applies only to charge type Discount; it does not affect appeasements, coupons, promotions, SnH, VAS, etc.
Indicates if price can be overridden for the item. Used by order capture systems such as point of sale to determine whether to allow users to override an item price.

Indicates if item requires an activation value to be entered (e.g. variable value gift cards). This attribute is used by point of sale to determine whether to prompt a user for item value upon scanning.
This attribute stores the link to item details page on the e-commerce site.If it is null, system will try to copy it from item.webURL
This column used by monitoring framework to set the time at which the immediate/next event has to occur
Total number of times fulfillment short was recieved

This field indicates the lifecycle of a released line cancellation to downstream fulfillment system. When the request is initiated then the status is Requested, when the line is successfully cancelled the status is updated to Success. If the fulfillment system rejects the cancellation for ever then the status is updated to Failure

Indicates if item can be shipped to an address. Will be captured from item selling attributes if it is null at the time of creating the order line. Any subsequent changes to this attribute in Item component will not be propagated.
In case of line split because of a permitted update after the parent line got partially released, the new line is linked to the parent line using this field.

Order line quantities cannot be released before this date and time. This value can be imported on the orderline, or it can be calculated by the system as a specified amount of time after an orderline has been confirmed. To specify the amount of time from UI, this can be configured on the orderlineremorseconfig.
This attribute is used to persist the sum of all the Order Line Informational Taxes.
Indicates if the order line is Reserved online pickup in store

The item tax department corresponds to the VAT rate and applicable to fiscal requirements. For Italy, we need to pass on this taxDepartment to the printer so that it can calculate taxes. For ex: taxDepartment 1 means 22%, 2 means 10% and so on.
Sum of cancelled taxes for the order line

Sum of cancelled items, charges, discounts, and taxes for the order line

Item ordered originally on the order line. This will be different from the order line item id in case of substitution by the fulfillment system.

Indicates virtual reservartion, i.e. reserved against a location which doesn''t maintain inventory

Indicates the Order line’s earliest ship date as predicted by Machine Learning. It is predicted based on historical data of time taken between order creation and order fulfilment. This attribute will be populated only when Machine learning is available.

Indicates the Order line’s earliest delivery date as predicted by Machine Learning. It is predicted based on historical data of time taken between order creation and order delivered. This attribute will be populated only when Machine learning is available.

Indicates the Order line’s earliest ship date as computed heuristically by the Promising component. Earliest ship date represents the earliest date that the (release) order can be shipped from the location, assuming that the order is released immediately after allocation occurs. The earliest ship date takes into account the Allocation date/time or Future Supply ETA, Location receipt and processing time, Location''s and Carrier''s operating hours and holidays.

Indicates the Order line’s earliest delivery date as computed heuristically by the Promising component. Earliest delivery date represents the date the (release) order can be delivered to the customer, assuming that the order is released immediately and shipped as soon as the location processes the order. This is calculated takes into account the Earliest ship date, Carrier zone transit time, Location''s and Carrier''s operating hours and holidays.

Indicates the location’s current backlog at the time of allocation. If Location’s capacity definition or capacity utilization level is not set then current backlog will not be tracked. Current backlog is measured in terms of orders, order lines or units based on the capacity utilization level defined for a location.
Indicates the order line ship to address id at the time of creating the allocation. It is updated only for ship to address lines if orderCaptureConfig.preventPostReleaseUpdate is configured to true.
Allocation ID of the originally ordered item in case of substitution. This field is only populated in the allocation of the substitution item to link it to the allocation of the ordered item.

Indicates if tax is included in the charge. Included in the requests sent to the tax engine, so that tax can be back-calculated from the charge amount. Used for charges which are inclusive of tax such as return SnH. For example, if return SnH is $10 and taxIncluded is true, then if the tax rate is 10%, the tax engine returns a tax of $1 if isTaxIncluded = false and the taxDetail is saved with isInformational = false. If isTaxIncluded = true, then the tax engine returns a tax of $.91 and the taxDetail is saved with isInformational = true.

Indicates if a charge applies only to return order lines, such as a restocking or return shipping charge. If true, then header return charges are prorated across order lines where isReturn is true. Return charges are displayed separately in the call center UI and are included in separate order total calculations as part of the order response. Return charges are taxed using the return order creation date, while non-return charges on return orders are taxed using the original order fulfillment date.
This attribute is intended to support stacked discounts. This attribute governs the sequence in which discounts are evaluated.
Contains the ChargeDetailId of the discount that is prorated at line  level

Indicates a discount has been prorated between unit price and other charges or between multiple charge details with the same relatedChargeType. This read-only field is updated during proration. E.g. an $11 discount is applied to a line with $100 unit price and $10 SnH. Two new charge details are be created to prorate the $11 into a $1 discount against SnH and a $10 discount against the unit price, so tax is calculated against the discounted unit price ($90) and SnH charge ($9). The $11 discount is updated with IsProratedAtSameLevel = true. Another example is a line with two VAS charges. If a VAS discount is applied, it’s prorated between these two VAS charges and is updated with IsProratedAtSameLevel = true.
Indicates if a charge applied after one or more quantity is returned.

Populated only for return invoices, and used by the payment component to transfer credit from the parent order to the return order. For example, a customer purchases an item in order A and then returns the item in order B, then order B has a return invoice with parent order id ''A''. When the payment component is transferring credit for the value of the return invoice, the credit is pulled from order A and applied to order B to be used as a refund or for exchange items.

Tracking number assigned to the package by the carrier. Used as the key to track packages in the third party carrier systems.

Carrier used to ship the package. Used by the delivery tracking services to select which carrier tracking API to call. Populated based on the fulfillment detail carrier, which gets created as a result of a ship event received from execution systems such as WM.
Field not used. Reserved for future use in delivery tracking.
Estimated delivery date provided by the carrier like FedEx or UPS for delivering the package for the assigned tracking number. Used to display the ETA for each package in the call center UI.

Field on the entity to store purge date)

Status of the package. If status is Open, then the package is shipped but not yet delivered, so the delivery tracking service continues to make calls to the carrier tracking API to check the status of the package. If the status is Closed, then the package has been delivered, and no additional calls to the carrier tracking API are made.

Unique identifier of the record. Can be imported, if an external system such as the carrier maintains a business key for each tracking detail. Otherwise, the system generates a unique hash using a combination of the date and status code.
Date when the package was scanned. Displayed on the call center UI.
City where the package was scanned. Displayed on the call center UI.
State where the package was scanned. Displayed on the call center UI.
Postal code of the location where the package was scanned. Displayed on the call center UI.
Description of the status logged by the carrier. Saved for informational purposes and to display in the call center UI. For example: ''Arrived at FedEx Location'' or ''Out for delivery''.

Status logged by the carrier. Saved for informational purposes, if the status is not delivered. If the status maps to delivered, then the corresponding quantity is moved to Delivered status and the header TrackingInfo record is closed.
Country where the package was scanned. Displayed on the call center UI.

The statusSubType field represents the sub status of current status field.For example, Status ''InTransit'' represents the package InTransit milestone in broader perspective, the precise milestone can be captured in the field statusSubType. For example, if the item is out for delivery, the broader status represents package as InTransit but the statusSubType can be OutForDelivery.
Description of the field statusSubType.
